title: Cynanchum stoloniferum
intro: Cynanchum stoloniferum, commonly known as Chinese foxglove or yellow foxglove, is a species of flowering plant in the Apocynaceae family. The plant is traditionally used in Chinese medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Cynanchum stoloniferum include glycosides, alkaloids, and flavonoids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal effects. Cynanchum stoloniferum is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and extracts, which are used to treat a range of health conditions and promote overall well-being.
taxonomy: Cynanchum stoloniferum is a species of flowering plant in the dogbane family Apocynaceae. This plant is native to Africa and is part of the Cynanchum genus, which comprises over 400 species of twining or climbing vines and shrubs. The genus Cynanchum is a member of the Asclepiadoideae subfamily, which is characterized by its unique flowers with hoods and a five-lobed corolla. The classification of Cynanchum stoloniferum reflects its evolutionary history as a member of the Apocynaceae family, which is thought to have originated over 100 million years ago. As a member of the Apocynaceae family, Cynanchum stoloniferum shares characteristics with other plants in this family, such as the presence of latex and a woody stem.
